jokes.text: Homer reading 'Recipes to think about in bed' - visual gag of inappropriate reading material
jokes.setup: Homer appears to be doing something productive
jokes.punchline: He's reading something absurdly inappropriate/sexual
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer sexual inappropriateness, economically delivered through visual

jokes.text: I'm going to sleep on the couch. / she sure loves that couch.
jokes.setup: Homer announces he's sleeping on the couch as if it's his choice
jokes.punchline: Marge's dry response reveals she kicked him out
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character voice - Marge's deadpan deflation of Homer's attempt to save face

jokes.text: oh, my god! I've shredded a child! Again!
jokes.setup: Willie accidentally hits someone with a kickball
jokes.punchline: The word 'Again!' reveals this is a recurring problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation - 'Again!' transforms accident into pattern of violence, perfectly captures Willie's character

jokes.text: venezuela, here I come!
jokes.setup: Willie has just injured a child again
jokes.punchline: His solution is to flee to South America
jokes.explanation: Absurd overreaction that fits Willie's extreme personality and immigrant background

jokes.text: Dodge-rock, volley-brick and base-game
jokes.setup: School offers alternative sports while ball is being repaired
jokes.punchline: All alternatives involve dangerous hard objects instead of balls
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that escalates the danger level while maintaining the rhythm of familiar sports names

jokes.text: I want to play dodge-rock! / You're out.
jokes.setup: Student eagerly volunteers for dangerous sport
jokes.punchline: Skinner immediately disqualifies them
jokes.explanation: Quick payoff to the dodge-rock setup, shows Skinner's protective authority

jokes.text: We'll be honeymooning at lake shelbyville lodge, cabin 32, room three. And we do not wish to be disturbed.
jokes.setup: Skinner announces wedding plans
jokes.punchline: Overly specific location details make it awkward and unsexy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner - turns romantic announcement into bureaucratic TMI

jokes.text: You finally hooked the skinfish.
jokes.setup: Student congratulates Mrs. Krabappel on engagement
jokes.punchline: Describes Skinner as a 'skinfish' - combining his name with unflattering fish metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant portmanteau that captures both Skinner's name and undesirable nature as a catch

jokes.text: It looks good... but I'm not going to celebrate till he's gaffed, gutted and hanging in my trophy room.
jokes.setup: Edna responds to fishing metaphor about catching Skinner
jokes.punchline: Extends metaphor to violent conclusion of trophy hunting
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation of fishing metaphor that reveals Edna's cynical view of marriage

jokes.text: sex with an alligator, screaming orgasms and virgin screaming orgasms
jokes.setup: Listing cocktail names at bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Increasingly sexual drink names, ending with the contradiction of 'virgin screaming orgasms'
jokes.explanation: Standard adult party humor with mild escalation and oxymoron payoff

jokes.text: I'm glad I sent the kids to grampa's to protect their innocent minds. / ..And here's some more germans we killed. That flame-thrower really toasted their waffles.
jokes.setup: Homer claims kids are protected at grandpa's
jokes.punchline: Cut to grandpa telling graphic war stories
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic juxtaposition with excellent visual editing between scenes

jokes.text: grampa, how do you sleep at night? / They drug us.
jokes.setup: Bart asks moral question about war stories
jokes.punchline: Grandpa answers literally about nursing home medication
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - takes expected moral question and answers with literal nursing home reality

jokes.text: It'S... another pair of edible panties! / now, that's sexual.
jokes.setup: Homer discovers gag gift
jokes.punchline: Moe's matter-of-fact pronouncement
jokes.explanation: Moe's blunt assessment captures his crude character

jokes.text: Would you please return my pants and/or underpants so I can go home?
jokes.setup: Bachelor party has gone wrong
jokes.punchline: Skinner's overly formal/bureaucratic way of asking for his clothes back
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner voice - turns embarrassing situation into administrative request

jokes.text: We're your buddies. Now, come on, homer's kids' principal, have a beer.
jokes.setup: Trying to be friendly to Skinner
jokes.punchline: Can't remember Skinner's name, identifies him by relationship to Homer's kids
jokes.explanation: Shows how peripheral Skinner is to Homer's drinking buddies

jokes.text: I can't-- I might be called upon to give directions later.
jokes.setup: Skinner refuses beer
jokes.punchline: Bizarre excuse about giving directions
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Skinner logic - takes responsibility for random potential emergencies

jokes.text: You were asked to chug-a-lug, and a-lug you shall chug!
jokes.setup: Forcing Skinner to drink
jokes.punchline: Homer's mock-Shakespearean command with beer terminology
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at authority using faux-formal language

jokes.text: Am I a good principal? / You're the best we could get with the funds at our disposal.
jokes.setup: Drunk Skinner seeks validation
jokes.punchline: Homer's backhanded 'compliment' about budget constraints
jokes.explanation: Brutal honest response that's simultaneously insulting and somehow supportive

jokes.text: delivering these ice-cold lady duffs has made me hot. Really hot.
jokes.setup: Duffman doing his promotional persona
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo that breaks character slightly
jokes.explanation: Standard Duffman character bit with mild sexual humor

jokes.text: 30 bucks an hour-- oh, yeah!
jokes.setup: Duffman performing
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals his wage in character
jokes.explanation: Breaks the illusion of his corporate mascot character by revealing business details

jokes.text: sorry to bother you, but we got an anonymous tip about loud music and a strip-da-didilly-dipper.
jokes.setup: Police arrive at party
jokes.punchline: It's Ned using his characteristic speech pattern to describe a stripper
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders voice - his prudish vocabulary applied to adult situation

jokes.text: hey, ladies, ladies, I-I really am a cop.
jokes.setup: Wiggum trying to assert authority
jokes.punchline: Nobody believes the real cop is actually a cop
jokes.explanation: Fits Wiggum's incompetent character - can't convince people of basic facts about himself

jokes.text: Uh, eddie, lou, how about a little backup here? / Hmm, hmm, like what you see, ladies?
jokes.setup: Wiggum calls for backup from his officers
jokes.punchline: His backup officers are also being mistaken for strippers
jokes.explanation: Escalates the confusion by making all the cops part of the mistaken identity

jokes.text: you know, I wish I had an exciting life, like that class picture photographer. / Oh! How many women has he had in that van? / Two that I know of.
jokes.setup: Homer envies photographer's exciting life with women
jokes.punchline: Barney's answer reveals very modest success
jokes.explanation: Deflates expectation of photographer's glamorous life with pathetic reality

jokes.text: No. Absolutely no friction dancing. / Oh, come on, you killjoy! Wussy. Grinch.
jokes.setup: Skinner refuses lap dance
jokes.punchline: Gets called progressively more emasculating names
jokes.explanation: Shows Skinner's prudishness and social awkwardness at bachelor party

jokes.text: Now you'll have someone who'll rub your back... without being asked. / Aah, not this again.
jokes.setup: Homer offers marriage advice about back rubs
jokes.punchline: Marge's reaction reveals this is his ongoing complaint
jokes.explanation: Reveals Homer's specific marital fixation and Marge's exasperation

jokes.text: I picked one that seemed far away, and it zoomed up like a june bug flying at my windshield.
jokes.setup: Skinner explains how wedding date arrived so quickly
jokes.punchline: Overly specific simile about june bugs and windshields
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward Skinner metaphor mixing nervous energy with mundane imagery

jokes.text: I could never lie to you guys. / Marge, guess what? Skinner wants to bail on his wedding.
jokes.setup: Skinner says he could never lie to his friends
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately betrays his confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - immediate betrayal with no self-awareness

jokes.text: Homer, you're still talking to me. / Oh, man, it's just awkward. I'm out of here.
jokes.setup: Marge points out Homer is still on the phone
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate awkward exit
jokes.explanation: Homer's social incompetence - doesn't know how to end phone conversations

jokes.text: Hey, you're the one always bugging me to screw up our marriage. Spice up! Spice up!
jokes.setup: Marge criticizes Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer blames Marge for wanting him to 'spice up' their marriage
jokes.explanation: Homer's twisted logic - blames Marge's attempts at improvement for his failures

jokes.text: Right, naked duffman? / Oh, uh... oh, yeah!
jokes.setup: Marge seeks support for marriage advice
jokes.punchline: She's asking a naked corporate mascot
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual of Marge consulting inappropriate source for relationship wisdom

jokes.text: Cowabunga one to fat load. / This is fat load. I'd like to request a new code name. / Denied.
jokes.setup: Military-style radio communication
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's wounded dignity over insulting code name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy character moment - his dignity conflicts with obvious physical description

jokes.text: That cloud looks awesome. That rock looks awesome. My hands look awesome.
jokes.setup: Otto compliments Mrs. Krabappel
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's just high and thinks everything looks awesome
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto character - deflates his compliment by revealing he's just stoned

jokes.text: Happy 50th anniversary, seymour. / I was thinking it might be time to combine our cd collections. / I don't know, edna. I'm not quite ready to take that plunge.
jokes.setup: Fantasy of future marriage milestone
jokes.punchline: Skinner still hesitant about minor commitment after 50 years
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Skinner's commitment phobia would persist even after 50 years of marriage

jokes.text: As I once said at the battle of khe sanh, 'I surrender.'
jokes.setup: Skinner compares marriage to military experience
jokes.punchline: Reveals he surrendered in Vietnam
jokes.explanation: Dark character revelation that explains both his military references and cowardly nature

jokes.text: I do. / no, you say that part later. I'm sorry, seymour.
jokes.setup: Minister asks if anyone objects to wedding
jokes.punchline: Edna misunderstands and gives wedding vows
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection on wedding ceremony structure

jokes.text: we got a runner. Snipers, take your shot. / I can't do it. That dress is a vera wang.
jokes.setup: Military operation to stop runaway bride
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy can't shoot because of expensive dress
jokes.explanation: Comic Book Guy's unexpected knowledge of fashion mixed with sniper scenario

jokes.text: is this corn oil? / Canola.
jokes.setup: Homer examines something at emotional wedding moment
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact correction about cooking oil
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of dramatic moment with domestic mundanity

jokes.text: He drove her to it you know. She was only doing it for the appliances. He's a she. She's a he. They're both, both.
jokes.setup: Gossip about the failed wedding
jokes.punchline: Escalating confusion about basic facts
jokes.explanation: Shows how quickly gossip becomes confused and contradictory

jokes.text: I spent $15 on those dance lessons, might as well use them. Forward, side, together. Back, side, together.
jokes.setup: Skinner's sad solo dance at his failed wedding
jokes.punchline: Methodically recites dance steps while alone
jokes.explanation: Perfectly pathetic Skinner moment - mechanical approach to romance even in heartbreak

jokes.text: Eh, bro's before ho'S.
jokes.setup: Nelson defending Skinner
jokes.punchline: School bully using crude expression to show male solidarity
jokes.explanation: Nelson's unexpected loyalty to authority figure through crude philosophy

jokes.text: Oh, passion's for teens and immigrants.
jokes.setup: Edna wants passion in marriage
jokes.punchline: Marge's cynical categorization of who gets passion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cynical observation about passion being for the young and desperate

jokes.text: Well, it'S... in my case... hmm...
jokes.setup: Edna asks what's the point of loveless marriage
jokes.punchline: Marge can't think of a good reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect beat showing Marge realizing the weakness of her own marriage defense

jokes.text: Wait a minute. I thought you didn't want to get married. / That was before I missed her smell, her warmth, her beautiful, beautiful penmanship.
jokes.setup: Homer confused about Skinner's change of heart
jokes.punchline: Skinner's list of what he misses gets increasingly specific and nerdy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character moment - finds romance in bureaucratic details like penmanship

jokes.text: every second without you is like a million kicks in the crotch. / Oh, that's so lovely.
jokes.setup: Homer feeds Skinner romantic line
jokes.punchline: Edna sarcastically responds to crude metaphor
jokes.explanation: Homer's terrible romantic advice contrasted with Edna's sarcastic response

jokes.text: There's no ring on that finger. / Uh-oh. Oh, why did I take it off? Oh, right. To see if I could skip it across lake michigan.
jokes.setup: Edna questions Homer's marriage credentials
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurd reason for removing wedding ring
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treats symbol of marriage as toy for ridiculous experiment

jokes.text: Which are you saying is dead? Our marriage or our batteries? Let's just say in an emergency, I wouldn't count on either one.
jokes.setup: Homer asks for clarification about what's dead
jokes.punchline: Marge's brutal assessment that both marriage and batteries are unreliable
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the battery metaphor with Marge's devastating honesty about both issues

jokes.text: Well, I was married once... in an online fantasy game. We were thinking of having children, but that would have severely drained my power crystals.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy admits to past marriage
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was virtual and explains not having kids in game terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy - treats virtual relationship with same seriousness as real marriage

jokes.text: We were thinking of having children, but that would have severely drained my power crystals.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy discusses his online marriage
jokes.punchline: His video game logic applied to procreation decisions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy line applying game mechanics to real life decisions with complete sincerity

jokes.text: We've made it to my car and you haven't left. A new record.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy on date with Edna
jokes.punchline: Celebrates minor milestone in his dating life
jokes.explanation: Perfectly pathetic Comic Book Guy moment showing his low expectations with women

jokes.text: Oh, I love your black tinted windows. / Actually, they're trash bags. Now, could you give me a pushing start?
jokes.setup: Edna admires car's windows
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy reveals they're trash bags and car doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Reveals Comic Book Guy's poverty and mechanical incompetence behind his pompous facade

jokes.text: uh-plee-ee-ee-eease settle for seymour / so plee-ee-ee-eease come back to the dork
jokes.setup: Skinner's musical plea to win Edna back
jokes.punchline: Self-aware lyrics calling himself a dork
jokes.explanation: Skinner's desperate self-aware plea that acknowledges his own inadequacy

jokes.text: My beloved is napping blissfully in the many folds of my spider-man t-shirt.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy describes Edna sleeping
jokes.punchline: Poetic description of his fat body hiding her
jokes.explanation: Beautifully grotesque image mixing romance with Comic Book Guy's physical reality

jokes.text: She is near-mint and comes from a very limited edition-- females who will talk to me.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy describes his affection for Edna
jokes.punchline: Uses comic book collecting terms to describe women
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character integration - applies his comic expertise to dating with devastating self-awareness

jokes.text: A fat, bald guy like you... you could have anyone.
jokes.setup: Homer argues Comic Book Guy should give up Edna
jokes.punchline: Describes negative qualities as if they're attractive
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete lack of self-awareness about his own similar physical traits

jokes.text: I wish you'd devote this much time and attention to our relationship. / Don't worry, marge. We'll get something to eat.
jokes.setup: Marge complains about lack of attention to their relationship
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misses her point and offers food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - treats emotional need as hunger

jokes.text: wow, the creator of futurama!
jokes.setup: Homer meets Matt Groening
jokes.punchline: Identifies him as creator of different show than The Simpsons
jokes.explanation: Meta joke where character doesn't recognize creator of his own show

jokes.text: I would kill the children of a thousand planets just to see you smile. / that is the most romantic thing I've ever heard, which is kind of sad if you think about it.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy's Klingon romantic declaration
jokes.punchline: Edna recognizes it's romantic while acknowledging how pathetic that is
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny commentary on both alien romance and Edna's low standards

jokes.text: Why are you dressed like catwoman? / You... oh, they told me it was catman!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy questions Skinner's costume
jokes.punchline: Skinner was tricked about costume identity
jokes.explanation: Shows Skinner's gullibility and lack of pop culture knowledge

jokes.text: Should we aid our brother in his blood feud ? / No. My mom worked really hard on this costume.
jokes.setup: Comic convention nerd considers joining fight
jokes.punchline: Won't risk mom's handiwork
jokes.explanation: Perfect nerd characterization - mommy issues override loyalty

jokes.text: It's like I'm dc comics, and you're marvel. / I understand completely.
jokes.setup: Edna explains incompatibility with comic metaphor
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy immediately understands this specific analogy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant breakup using Comic Book Guy's expertise - he understands comics better than relationships

jokes.text: You look like a malaysian transsexual.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy's mom sees him in costume
jokes.punchline: Delivers highly specific insult
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific put-down that fits the character's harsh treatment of her son

jokes.text: Marge, you just agreed to raise the kids klingon! / D-ohh!
jokes.setup: Klingon wedding ceremony in alien language
jokes.punchline: Homer reveals what Marge unknowingly agreed to
jokes.explanation: Pays off the alien wedding setup with unexpected parenting consequences

jokes.text: Marge, you just agreed to raise the kids klingon! D-ohh!
jokes.setup: The Klingon officiant conducts the ceremony in his language
jokes.punchline: Homer realizes Marge agreed to alien child-rearing practices
jokes.explanation: Good callback with language barrier comedy creating unexpected marital obligations
